name: Brecon Beacons Dark Sky Reserve
description: Wales's Brecon Beacons National Park became the first International Dark Sky Reserve in Wales and one of only a handful worldwide. Despite being relatively close to Cardiff and Swansea, the park's mountainous terrain and active light-management programs maintain dark conditions in the core zones. The reserve has become a model for balancing dark sky preservation with proximity to urban populations, demonstrating that meaningful dark sky access can be preserved even in densely populated regions.
